  • Home
  • Hard Drives & SSDs
  • Solid State Drives (SSD)

Solid State Drives (SSD)

A solid state drive acts as your computer's long-term memory. It stores and remembers files even when your device is off. SSDs are faster, more durable, energy-efficient and save battery life on portable devices.
 
Browse Scorptec's solid state drive catalogue
If you're looking for a solid state drive, check out these top items from Scorptec's wide selection of SSDs. You can use these to upgrade your current rig or even purchase an external solid state drive.
●    Samsung 970 EVO Plus 1TB M.2 NVMe SSD — It features Samsung's newest V-NAND technology and firmware optimisation.
●    Samsung 980 PRO 1TB M.2 PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSD — This well-known SSD uses the PCIe 4.0 interface to deliver twice the data transmission speed of PCIe 3.0.
 

Trust Scorptec for reliable shipping and secure payment options
At Scorptec, we offer standard and express shipping delivery. You can monitor the progress of your SSD order and shipment with the tracking number we provide.
We accept a variety of secure online and in-store payment methods, including credit card, PayPal, Afterpay, Klarna and Zip.
 

Get the best solid state drive from Scorptec
Scorptec — Australia's largest I.T. retailer — stocks a wide range of high-quality solid state drives, components, graphic cards, accessories and other PC hardware and software. You can rest assured knowing that we carry the SSD that you need, whether it’s an M.2 SSD or NVMe SSD. We also have SSDs that fit other systems, from PS5 SSDs to Samsung SSDs. You can find SSDs with varying storage available in our store, from 500GB SSDs and 1TB SSDs to 2TB and 4TB SSDs. 

We have over 10,000 offerings in our inventory, so we're sure to have what you need. That also includes other hard drives and SSD products and accessories, such as external drives, docks and enclosures. 
Browse our catalogue today to find the right tech product for you. For enquiries, please feel free to contact our team.
 

FAQs
What are the benefits of using an SSD over a traditional hard drive?
Solid state drives offer several advantages over traditional HDDs. The key benefits include faster data access speeds, improved system responsiveness, enhanced durability due to no moving parts and reduced power consumption. SSDs are the go-to choice for those seeking a significant boost in overall system performance.

How do SSDs improve computer performance?
SSDs excel in speed, thanks to their flash memory technology. Unlike HDDs, which rely on spinning disks and mechanical arms to read/write data, SSDs use NAND-based flash memory for near-instantaneous data access. This translates to quicker boot times, faster application launches and overall snappier system responsiveness.

What factors should be considered when choosing an SSD for gaming?
For a gaming SSD, consider the following factors:
●    Capacity — Aim for a capacity that accommodates your game library and operating system.
●    Read/write speeds — Faster speeds lead to reduced loading times in games.
●    Durability — Choose SSDs with features like wear levelling to ensure longevity.
●    Brand and reliability — Opt for reputable brands known for reliable SSDs.
 

How does SSD capacity affect its performance and price?
SSD capacity impacts both performance and price. Larger capacities often provide better performance, as they can distribute data across more memory chips. However, larger capacities come at a higher cost. It's essential to strike a balance based on your storage needs and budget.
 

Can SSDs be used on any computer or laptop?
Yes, SSDs are versatile and compatible with most computers and laptops. They come in various form factors, including 2.5-inch, M.2 and PCIe, allowing compatibility with a wide range of systems. Before purchasing, ensure your system supports the specific SSD form factor you're considering.
 

What is the lifespan of an SSD, and how does it compare to HDDs?
While SSDs and HDDs both have finite lifespans, SSDs typically last longer due to their lack of moving parts. The lifespan is measured in write cycles and modern SSDs can withstand thousands of cycles. In practical terms, an SSD's lifespan often exceeds the average lifespan of an HDD.
 

How do I install an SSD on my computer?
Installing an SSD is a straightforward process. Just follow the steps below:
1.    Prepare and back up — Before you start, back up your data to prevent any loss. Shut down your computer and disconnect it from the power source.
2.    Access the internal components — If you're installing the SSD internally, open your computer case. Most cases have screws or latches to facilitate easy access.
3.    Connecting a SATA SSD — Locate an available SATA port on your motherboard. Then, connect the SSD to the port using a SATA cable. You can provide power by connecting a SATA power connector.
4.    Secure the SSD — Secure the SSD in place using screws. Many cases have dedicated slots for SSDs or designated areas in drive bays.
5.    Close the case — Once the SSD is securely in place, close your computer case, ensuring it's properly sealed.
6.    Boot up — Power on your computer and access the BIOS/UEFI settings to ensure the SSD is recognised. Set it as the primary boot drive if necessary.
7.    Check recognition — Confirm that your SSD is recognised by checking your system information or device manager.
 

What are the differences between SATA and NVMe SSDs?
SATA and NVMe SSDs differ primarily in speed and form factor. NVMe, utilising PCIe technology, offers significantly faster read/write speeds compared to SATA, making it optimal for demanding tasks like gaming and content creation. NVMe SSDs typically come in M.2 or PCIe card form factors, while SATA SSDs use the traditional 2.5-inch form factor. When choosing between them, be sure to keep in mind your performance needs and motherboard compatibility.
 

Can SSDs be used for external storage?
Absolutely. External SSDs provide the speed advantages of internal SSDs in a portable form. They connect via USB or Thunderbolt, making them suitable for tasks like high-speed data transfers and running applications directly from the external drive.
 

How do I maintain the performance and health of my SSD?
To ensure your SSD remains in peak condition, follow the tips below:
●    Firmware updates — Keep your SSD firmware updated for performance improvements and bug fixes.
●    Trim support — Enable TRIM, a feature that helps maintain SSD performance over time.
●    Regular backups — Regularly back up your data to prevent loss in case of unforeseen issues.
●    Avoid overfilling — Keep a portion of your SSD free to allow for efficient performance.
●    Temperature control — Maintain a cool operating temperature for your SSD to prevent overheating.
